summary: The team discussed building a restaurant app where customers vote on menu items to optimize offerings. Key focus areas included a simple voting system, lightweight authentication, and a dual interface for customers and staff. Data tracking and analytics were emphasized to support inventory and decision-making. UX simplicity and mobile performance were considered critical. Next steps involve consolidating requirements for further refinement.

details: Customers vote or rate menu items
Menu evolves based on popularity
Feedback loop to refine offerings
Voting must be quick and simple (1–2 taps)
Options include thumbs up/down, stars, or reactions
Encourage voting via subtle prompts
Consider lightweight login (Google/Apple)
Anonymous voting risks spam and unreliable data
Balance between low friction and data reliability
Track ratings over time for trends and predictions
Identify top and low-performing dishes
Potential for demand forecasting and inventory optimization
Alerts for consistently underperforming dishes
Link dish popularity to ingredient usage
Suggest optimal stock levels based on demand
Requires backend mapping of dishes to ingredients
Dual interface: customer-facing app and staff dashboard
Customers see menu and ratings only
Staff access ingredients, inventory, and analytics
Backend must be scalable
Home/About page
Menu display
Contact information
Location with map

decisions: Adopt a simple and fast voting mechanism
Use lightweight authentication instead of fully anonymous voting
Implement dual interfaces for customers and staff
Restrict ingredient visibility to staff only
Prioritize analytics and data tracking from the beginning

action_items.task: Define backend requirements for ingredient tracking and scalability
Design simple and intuitive voting UX
Outline analytics metrics and data tracking strategy
Explore methods to encourage user engagement in voting
Evaluate notification feature for future phase

open_questions.question: What specific voting format will be implemented (thumbs, stars, reactions)?
What level of authentication detail is required to balance usability and data integrity?
How exactly will ingredient-to-dish mapping be structured in the backend?
What mechanisms will be most effective in encouraging users to vote?
When should notification features be introduced (phase timeline)?
